channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A 'coalition of the willing' mulls more aid for Ukraine and a possible armed force
item.description: Ukraine’s allies from across Europe have gathered for talks in Paris about how to strengthen Kyiv’s hand and its military as it pushes for a ceasefire with Russia.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester And Samuel Petrequin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron said Wednesday that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US gives initial approval for Qatar to purchase MQ-9 armed drones for nearly $2 billion
item.description: The United States has given an initial approval for the energy-rich Mideast nation of Qatar to buy eight armed MQ-9B Predator drones for its military, an estimated purchase worth nearly $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday and held talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: Police have used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey's capital, potentially reigniting tensions after two days of relative calm in the country's biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices on Wednesday unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, and ordered him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio: 'Someone made a big mistake' with Jeffrey Goldberg invite to Signal chat
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday said “someone made a big mistake” in adding the editor in chief of The Atlantic into a group chat with the Trump administration’s top national security officials discussing an attack plan on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Canada bars Tesla from rebate program over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canada has barred electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla from all of its rebate programs and banned the Texas-based automotive company from future electric vehicle rebate programs over President Trump’s tariff back-and-forth with Ottawa.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: NATO is clarifying comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: German authorities raid group that allegedly aims to overthrow Eritrean government
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Police were executing raids in six German states Wednesday against a group that authorities say “is part of an international network whose aim is to overthrow the government in Eritrea.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s truth commission says government responsible for fraud and abuse in foreign adoptions
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s truth commission concluded the government bears responsibility for facilitating a foreign adoption program rife with fraud and abuse, driven by efforts to reduce welfare costs and enabled by private agencies that often manipulated children’s backgrounds and or
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai prime minister survives a challenge by rivals who say her father is pulling the strings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra easily survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday, following a two-day debate in which rivals charged that she has mismanaged the country and let her father, a former prime minister, control her administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says it brokered deal to end fighting in the Black Sea in talks with Ukraine and Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The United States said Tuesday that it had reached a tentative agreement for Ukraine and Russia to stop fighting and ensure safe navigation in the Black Sea in separate talks with both sides, but many details were unresolved, and the Kremlin made the deal conditional on lifting
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Ukraine agree to Black Sea ceasefire following US talks
item.description: The Kremlin suggested it may not honor the terms of a Black Sea ceasefire with Ukraine until Russia is admitted back into the SWIFT international banking system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey jails Istanbul mayor before trial, protests rage throughout city
item.description: A Turkish court jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, pending a trial on corruption charges, in a move likely to cause the country's biggest protests in more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis to be released from hospital Sunday after 5 weeks fighting life-threatening pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday after 38 days battling a severe case of pneumonia in both lungs that threatened his life on two occasions and raised the prospect — for now put to rest — of a papal resignation or funeral.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, TRISHA THOMAS and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Taliban release an American man who was abducted while traveling in Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American man who was abducted more than two years ago while traveling through Afghanistan as a tourist has been released by the Taliban in a deal with the Trump administration that Qatari negotiators helped broker, the State Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New skeletons challenge assumptions about ancient Egypt
item.description: The skeletons found laid to rest alongside nobles has challenged assumptions about Egyptian burial practices.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation
